mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC below 0.02 during rate decline periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and impending consumer spending contraction, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE crosses above 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicate a synchronized housing market contraction that reduces construction activity and consumer wealth, driving negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint down-trends in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 20-day returns with a statistically significant negative coefficient (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects robust manufacturing capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield flat or negative 60-day forward returns during late-cycle phases, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 over 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates exceeding 5.0 percent impose severe financing constraints on highly leveraged firms and reduce growth stock valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS remains above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and drives immediate positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE shows no predictive power for 5-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02) or produces negative returns in more than 50 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth reflects broad-based economic demand that supports corporate revenue expansion and earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60 trading day horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 10-year sample, or Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 100 index baseline indicates a contraction in manufacturing output and supply chain bottlenecks that disrupts corporate operations and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of periods when INDPRO drops below 100, or the signal loses predictive significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.
